📘 Glossary — what every number means

Temperature (0–100)

The composite "how hot/late" score. Each signal is ranked vs its own trailing 5y history (0=coldest ever, 100=hottest ever) AND vs cluster peers, then blended 50/50. Bucket scores average their signals; composite averages buckets. High temperature ⇒ stretched positioning + price-revealed sentiment, historically associated with negative forward returns at extremes (V1.5 backtest IC −0.020 at 3m fwd, bot decile hit 56%).

Bucket scores (Pos / Tech)

Each 0–100, average of underlying signals (each signal a percentile vs own history & cluster peers).

  • Pos — Positioning (insider Form 4, FINRA short volume, NASDAQ true SI days-to-cover)
  • Tech — Technical/momentum: sentiment expressed via price action (returns, RSI, distance from 200d MA, % from 52w high)

NTM P/E (price ÷ forward consensus EPS) is shown on per-ticker drill-downs as overlay context only — NOT in the composite. Tool measures sentiment / positioning only; valuation is fundamental analysis done separately. No TTM multiples are used — only NTM (forward) per design choice.

Conviction (0–100)

How much the buckets agree. High conviction = all buckets pointing same way (all hot, or all cold). Low = mixed signals (e.g., expensive valuation but cold technicals — value-trap risk).

Anomaly count

Number of signals where this name is at the 90th+ percentile vs its cluster peers today. High = stands out from peers across many measures.

7d Δ (temperature change)

Today's temperature minus 5 trading days ago. Red/positive = heating up. Green/negative = cooling off (contrarian-favorable).

Compound flags

  • 🔥 Late — Positioning ≥ 85 AND Technical ≥ 85. Both buckets triple-extreme.
  • ❄️ Washout — Positioning ≤ 15 AND Technical ≤ 15. Both buckets triple-extreme washed.
  • 📅 Earnings — earnings reporting within next 14 days.

Color coding (contrarian)

Red = hot/extended/dangerous. Green = cold/washed-out/opportunity. Inverted from typical price-momentum coloring because this tool is contrarian.

Backtest validation (V1.5)

Composite IC −0.020 at 3-month forward (Spearman). Bottom-decile temperature → 56% positive forward return; top-decile → 54% negative. Strongest individual signal: si_true_dtc (NASDAQ days-to-cover) IC −0.064 at 3m. V1.5 dropped valuation from composite without meaningful loss vs V1.4 (was −0.022).

Methodology

Universe: 366 TMT names (mcap ≥ $1.5B) drawn from theme_detector.

Signals: 18 daily signals — 13 in composite, 5 overlay-only. Inclusion driven by backtest IC sign (positive IC = trend, excluded from contrarian composite).

Dual percentile: each signal ranked vs (a) own 5y trailing history and (b) cluster peers cross-section. Bucket scores average those.

Composite: weighted average of bucket scores. Reweighted when buckets are missing. Min 2 buckets required for a temperature reading.

Backtest: 10y daily panel. IC = Spearman correlation between signal percentile and forward return. V1.5 composite IC −0.020 at 3m fwd, decile spread −2.09%, bot decile hit 56%. (V1.4 with valuation included was −0.022 — statistically equivalent.) See data/backtest_report.md.

Limitations: options bucket not implemented (Polygon $200/mo would unlock). ETF flows forward-only. EPS revisions live snapshot only. NASDAQ true SI covers only NASDAQ-listed names (~65% of universe). 13F has 45-day reporting lag and is long-only.
